title: rosa canina f. macroacantha
intro: Rosa canina f. macroacantha, also known as the prickly rose or wild rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa canina plant that is characterized by its distinctive, long thorns. The medicinal properties of Rosa canina f. macroacantha include its high content of vitamin C, flavonoids, and essential fatty acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. This plant has been traditionally used to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and support cardiovascular well-being, with benefits including improved wound healing, reduced symptoms of menopause, and enhanced immune function. Herbal preparations of Rosa canina f. macroacantha include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make rosehip syrup or powder, and are often combined with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
